text: A global memory chip shortage driven by the AI boom has sent RAM prices surging roughly ninety percent in the first quarter of twenty twenty-six.
Only three companies manufacture RAM worldwide, Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron, and all have shifted production toward more profitable AI memory chips.
Apple CEO Tim Cook has warned that memory pricing will increase significantly, while HP and Dell are already raising their PC prices.
The crisis has hit smaller companies hardest, with over one hundred ninety thousand small businesses struggling as the market shifts to an hourly pricing model.
IDC projects PC shipments could drop up to eight point nine percent this year, while smartphone shipments may fall five point two percent as manufacturers pass rising costs to consumers.
The AI revolution is creating winners and losers in the tech supply chain.
While data center operators race to secure memory for AI training, everyday consumers face higher prices for basic electronics, turning a tech boom into a hidden tax on everyone.
